title: A song I sing in the shower
summary: Name a song you sing in the shower and describe how it makes you feel.
topic: A song I sing in the shower
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a song you sing in the shower. Say what the song is, who sings it, and how it makes you feel. Use present simple and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: The song I sing in the shower is…
When I sing it, I feel…
sampleText: The song I always sing in the shower is called 'Happy' by Pharrell Williams. I first heard it in a café in Cape Town, and I loved it immediately. The tune is very catchy and the rhythm makes me want to move. The lyrics are simple and easy to remember. I sing the chorus very loudly in the bathroom. My roommate says she can hear me from the kitchen, but I do not care. When I sing it, I feel energetic and ready for the day. Sometimes I sing it twice because it is so short and upbeat. The song is about feeling happy even when things are hard. That is a message I like. I also listen to it on my phone when I clean the flat. The song is not new, but for me it never gets old. On bad days, this song helps me a lot. I think music in the morning is a great way to start.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The song I always sing in the shower
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Bài hát tôi luôn hát trong lúc tắm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the rhythm makes me want to move
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhịp điệu khiến tôi muốn nhún nhảy
không bao giờ lỗi thời, mãi hay

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for likes and habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn cho thói quen và cảm xúc thường trực: 'I always sing', 'I feel energetic', 'I like'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for habits and ongoing feelings: 'I always sing', 'I feel energetic', 'The song is about feeling happy'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Verb 'make' + object + infinitive
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'make somebody do something' = khiến ai đó làm gì: 'the rhythm makes me want to move'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'make + object + base verb': 'the rhythm makes me want to move', 'this song helps me a lot'.

sampleTranslationVi: Bài hát tôi luôn hát trong lúc tắm có tên là 'Happy' của Pharrell Williams. Tôi nghe nó lần đầu trong một quán cà phê ở Cape Town và yêu thích ngay lập tức. Giai điệu rất bắt tai và nhịp điệu khiến tôi muốn nhún nhảy. Lời bài hát đơn giản và dễ nhớ. Tôi hát điệp khúc rất to trong phòng tắm. Bạn cùng phòng nói cô ấy có thể nghe thấy tôi từ bếp, nhưng tôi không quan tâm. Khi hát nó, tôi cảm thấy tràn đầy năng lượng và sẵn sàng cho ngày mới. Đôi khi tôi hát hai lần vì nó rất ngắn và sôi động. Bài hát nói về việc cảm thấy hạnh phúc ngay cả khi mọi thứ khó khăn. Đó là thông điệp tôi thích. Tôi cũng nghe nó trên điện thoại khi dọn dẹp căn hộ. Bài hát không mới, nhưng với tôi nó không bao giờ lỗi thời. Vào những ngày tệ, bài hát này giúp tôi rất nhiều. Tôi nghĩ âm nhạc buổi sáng là cách tuyệt vời để bắt đầu.

mcQuestions.prompt: What is the name of the song?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The song … is called Happy by Pharrell Williams'.
mcQuestions.hint: The first sentence names the song.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where did the writer first hear the song?
options: In a café in Cape Town.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I first heard it in a café in Cape Town'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the location in the second sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: Who can hear the writer singing?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'My roommate says she can hear me from the kitchen'.
mcQuestions.hint: Someone else lives in the flat.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the writer feel when singing the song?
options: Energetic and ready for the day.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I feel energetic and ready for the day'.

options: Be happy even when things are hard.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The song is about feeling happy even when things are hard'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'easy to remember' (for a tune)?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'catchy' describes a tune that sticks in your head.
mcQuestions.hint: It's an adjective about how easy a tune is to remember.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence places the adverb correctly?
options: I sing loudly always the chorus.
Always I sing the chorus loudly.
I always sing the chorus loudly.
I sing the chorus always loudly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' sits before the main verb: 'I always sing'.
mcQuestions.hint: Frequency adverbs go before the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: The writer also listens to the song when they do what?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I also listen to it on my phone when I clean the flat'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the second activity the writer does with the song.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means full of energy?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'energetic' means having or showing a lot of energy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'make + object + verb' correctly?
options: The rhythm makes me to move.
The rhythm makes me want to move.
The rhythm making me want to move.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'make + object + base verb' (no 'to' after 'make', but 'want to' is one unit here).

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer sometimes sing the song twice?
options: Because it is so short and upbeat.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I sing it twice because it is so short and upbeat'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do many people feel happier when they listen to or sing music in the morning?
